description_long: This object is a vintage floor-standing television set with a wooden cabinet exterior, featuring a large, convex glass screen framed by a dark bezel. The front panel includes two prominent rotary dials and a central metallic control bar, likely for channel and volume adjustment, along with a fabric speaker grille below. The sides are smooth, light wood panels with a subtle sheen, while the back is a darker, textured panel with gold-printed branding and technical information. The television stands on four slender, black-tipped legs, elevating it above the ground. The overall design is boxy and robust, typical of mid-20th-century televisions, with a focus on both function and decorative appeal. The object exudes a retro aesthetic, making it a recognizable piece in urban or domestic settings.
description: A vintage wooden floor-standing television with a convex glass screen, rotary dials, and a fabric speaker grille, elevated on four slender legs. The design is boxy and retro, with a mix of light wood and dark accents.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, two rotary dials, a central metallic control bar, and a fabric speaker grille.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying a smooth, light wood panel with a subtle sheen and no visible controls or features.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a dark, textured back panel with gold-printed branding and technical information.
description_view_3: Right side view showing another smooth, light wood panel, mirroring the left side with no visible controls.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, dark gray bezel with a matte finish, slightly rounded corners, and a robust, retro appearance.
attribute_control_button_layout: Front panel, two rotary dials and a central metallic control bar arranged horizontally below the screen.
